forecastIntelligence.explainabilitySummary: Supplier payment timing is the main pressure on cash, while revenue opportunity pipeline is the strongest lever the owner can control.

decisionCallouts.title: Why cash dips on 2026-07-18
decisionCallouts.answer: -£9,700 net movement on 2026-07-18, driven by £9,700 of outflows against £0 of inflows.
evidence: £11,500 authorised supplier bills in the model
£15,190 authorised receivables depend on payment timing
Safe threshold first breached on 2026-07-18
decisionCallouts.businessDecision: Prioritise actions that move cash before the minimum-cash date, not actions that arrive after the danger window.

decisionCallouts.title: Which 3 factors move cash most
decisionCallouts.answer: Revenue opportunity pipeline: £21,941 | Approved action lift: £13,983 | Supplier payment timing: £11,500
evidence: 7 growth opportunity/opportunities
3 cash action(s) selected
£11,500 authorised supplier bills
decisionCallouts.businessDecision: Focus the owner conversation on the few timing levers that actually change runway.

decisionCallouts.title: What changed after actions
decisionCallouts.answer: £13,933 improvement to minimum cash; crunch probability moves from 100% to 0%.
evidence: 3 cash-flow action(s) included
After-action forecast removes the threshold breach
Offer Brightside Studios a 2% early-payment incentive
decisionCallouts.businessDecision: Approve the smallest action bundle that removes the breach and preserves supplier/customer trust.

decisionCallouts.title: Monte Carlo means many realistic payment futures
decisionCallouts.answer: At the minimum-cash date, simulated outcomes range from -£2,650 to £4,700.
evidence: 420 seeded simulations vary customer payment delay around contact reliability
p10-p90 band shows the middle 80% of realistic outcomes
The red line is the deterministic expected timing case
decisionCallouts.businessDecision: Treat the forecast as a risk range, not a single promise; approve actions that work in pessimistic timing cases.

timeSeriesDiagnostics.label: Daily ledger model
timeSeriesDiagnostics.detail: Every day rolls opening cash plus dated receivables, supplier bills, and recurring flows into closing cash.
timeSeriesDiagnostics.method: Time-indexed deterministic ledger from Xero due dates, planned payment dates, expected payment dates, and recurring cash flows.

timeSeriesDiagnostics.label: Worst 7-day net cash movement
timeSeriesDiagnostics.detail: 2026-07-12 to 2026-07-18 is the most stressful seven-day run in the baseline forecast.
timeSeriesDiagnostics.method: Rolling seven-day window over daily closing-minus-opening cash movement.

timeSeriesDiagnostics.label: Average daily cash velocity
timeSeriesDiagnostics.detail: Daily movement volatility is £1,644, which is why timing changes matter more than the average.
timeSeriesDiagnostics.method: Mean and standard deviation of daily net cash movements across the forecast horizon.

timeSeriesDiagnostics.label: Payment timing uncertainty
timeSeriesDiagnostics.detail: On 2026-07-18, the simulated p10-p90 balance range is -£2,650 to £4,700.
timeSeriesDiagnostics.method: Monte Carlo simulation using contact payment reliability and invoice timing variance.

models.name: Daily cash ledger forecast
models.purpose: Project daily opening and closing cash over 30-90 days.
models.method: Deterministic time-series ledger with dated inflows and outflows.

models.name: Customer payment-delay model
models.purpose: Estimate when receivables will actually arrive.
models.method: Contact-level median days late and reliability scoring from Xero payment history.

models.name: Monte Carlo cash simulation
models.purpose: Estimate probability of a future cash threshold breach.
models.method: 420 seeded simulations varying customer payment delays around contact reliability.

models.name: Cash-driver attribution
models.purpose: Explain which business factors move the forecast most.
models.method: Sensitivity tests and factor scoring against the minimum cash point.
models.output: 6 ranked cash drivers explain the forecast movement.

cashDrivers.label: Revenue opportunity pipeline
cashDrivers.explanation: Detected growth opportunities do not fix today's bank balance immediately, but they improve the forward cash outlook.
evidence: 7 growth opportunity/opportunities
£25,450 expected revenue impact
Invoice closed-won Conversion Sprint
cashDrivers.sensitivity: Converting the top opportunity adds about £5,590 expected cash-flow lift.

cashDrivers.label: Approved action lift
cashDrivers.explanation: The selected cash actions change the timing of receipts and payments before the risk window arrives.
evidence: 3 cash action(s) selected
£13,933 improvement to minimum cash
After-action forecast removes the threshold breach
cashDrivers.sensitivity: Removing selected actions would bring the minimum cash back to -£2,650.

cashDrivers.label: Supplier payment timing
cashDrivers.explanation: Supplier bills and planned payment dates create the steepest downward steps in the short-term cash curve.
evidence: £11,500 authorised supplier bills
3 payable item(s) in the forecast window
One supplier timing action is available for approval
cashDrivers.sensitivity: Pulling supplier payments 7 days earlier lowers minimum cash by £0.

cashDrivers.label: Starting cash buffer
cashDrivers.explanation: Opening cash relative to the safety threshold determines how much timing shock the business can absorb.
evidence: Opening cash: £12,600
Safe threshold: £5,000
Baseline breach: 2026-07-18
cashDrivers.sensitivity: Every £1,000 of extra starting cash delays or softens threshold risk.

cashDrivers.label: Fixed cost load
cashDrivers.explanation: Recurring rent, software, and operating costs reduce the buffer even when invoice timing is stable.
evidence: 2 recurring outflow(s)
£3,750 recurring outflow per cycle
Recurring flows are projected across the 90-day ledger
cashDrivers.sensitivity: A 10% fixed-cost reduction would improve monthly cash by about £375.

cashDrivers.label: Customer payment timing
cashDrivers.explanation: The forecast is most sensitive to when authorised receivables actually land, not just their due dates.
evidence: £15,190 open customer receivables
3 authorised customer invoice(s)
Largest invoice: INV-4018 from Brightside Studios
cashDrivers.sensitivity: If open customer receipts slip by 7 days, minimum cash falls by £0.

recommendedActions.title: Offer Brightside Studios a 2% early-payment incentive
recommendedActions.rationale: The discount costs £157 but accelerates a large invoice before the crunch date.
recommendedActions.messageDraft: Hi Brightside Studios, I hope you are well. We are offering a 2% early-payment discount on INV-4018 if payment can be made this week. Would that be helpful on your side?

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Add an approved early-payment offer note and queue the customer email; invoice totals stay unchanged until payment is confirmed.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner approves the discount language and can cap the offer window.

recommendedActions.title: Chase Acme Retail Group
recommendedActions.rationale: Acme Retail Group usually pays 16 days late, so a targeted reminder can pull INV-4012 into the risk window.
recommendedActions.messageDraft: Hi Acme Retail Group, I hope you are well. A quick reminder that INV-4012 for £4,200 is now due. Could you let us know when payment is scheduled?

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Create a contact note and queue the invoice follow-up draft against the Xero invoice record.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner reviews the customer message before anything is sent.

recommendedActions.title: Delay PrintCo Supplies by 7 days
recommendedActions.rationale: PrintCo Supplies has previously accepted short payment extensions, and this bill lands inside the crunch window.
recommendedActions.messageDraft: Hi PrintCo Supplies, we are reviewing payment timing for BILL-188. Would it be possible to move payment to 2026-07-25? We value the relationship and want to confirm this works for your team.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Queue a supplier extension request and record the approved payment-plan note against the bill.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms supplier sensitivity before the request is sent.

revenueOpportunities.title: Invoice closed-won Conversion Sprint
revenueOpportunities.recommendedAction: Create a draft Xero invoice for the closed-won CRM deal before the cash-risk window arrives.
evidence: MockCRM deal CRM-DEAL-6500 is closed-won for £6,500.
Smart Mapper matched Brightside Studio Ltd to Brightside Studios with 98% confidence.
No similar Xero invoice exists for the matched contact, amount, and close-date window.
revenueOpportunities.messageDraft: Hi Brightside Studios, thanks again for confirming the conversion sprint. I have prepared the draft invoice for review and can adjust the details before it is sent.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Create a draft Xero invoice payload and queue the customer follow-up for owner review.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms scope, tax/VAT treatment, and invoice timing before any Xero writeback or email send.

revenueOpportunities.title: Reactivate Harbor Coffee Co
revenueOpportunities.recommendedAction: Send a personalised check-in with a relevant follow-on offer from their last purchase.
evidence: Harbor Coffee Co previously generated £8,400 of revenue.
No paid invoice has landed for 108 days.
Average historical invoice value is £8,400.
revenueOpportunities.messageDraft: Hi Harbor Coffee Co, we loved working with you on the last project. We spotted a few quick wins that could build on that work and would be happy to share a short plan this week.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Create a contact note, queue a reactivation task, and attach the owner-approved outreach draft.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner approves the offer angle before the customer is contacted.

revenueOpportunities.title: Convert Luna Fitness to a Content retainer
revenueOpportunities.recommendedAction: Offer a quarterly retainer that turns repeated ad-hoc invoices into predictable recurring revenue.
evidence: Luna Fitness bought Content work 3 times recently.
Average invoice value is £1,967.
A subscription improves revenue predictability and cash-flow visibility.
revenueOpportunities.messageDraft: Hi Luna Fitness, we noticed the monthly content work has become a regular rhythm. Would a fixed monthly retainer make planning and approvals easier on your side?

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Draft a retainer proposal and prepare a Xero repeating-invoice template for owner review.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms scope, price, and cadence before any recurring invoice is created.

revenueOpportunities.title: Cross-sell Conversion to Brightside Studios
revenueOpportunities.recommendedAction: Send a value-led recommendation connected to the service they already bought.
evidence: Brightside Studios has recent revenue of £7,840.
They bought Website but not Conversion.
Cross-sell is tied to an adjacent service, not a generic promotion.
revenueOpportunities.messageDraft: Hi Brightside Studios, based on the recent work, the next highest-leverage step is a focused conversion sprint. We can keep it lightweight and tie it directly to the outcomes from the current project.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Prepare a draft Xero quote for the next-best service and queue the personalised recommendation.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner approves pricing and scope before the quote is sent.

revenueOpportunities.title: Repackage underperforming Conversion offer
revenueOpportunities.recommendedAction: Bundle the underperforming offer with a better-selling adjacent service and test it with recent buyers.
evidence: Conversion revenue is £4,200, below the portfolio average of £8,773.
1 invoice(s) include this service.
The action is a pricing/packaging experiment, not an accounting adjustment.
revenueOpportunities.messageDraft: Internal action: repackage conversion as an add-on to a proven service, then test the offer with customers who recently bought adjacent work.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Create an internal pricing task and prepare a draft Xero item/quote bundle for review.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner decides whether the service should be repriced, bundled, or retired.

revenueOpportunities.title: Protect booked revenue from Acme Retail Group
revenueOpportunities.recommendedAction: Trigger a customer-specific follow-up before the payment delay hits the forecast.
evidence: INV-4012 has £4,200 still due.
Acme Retail Group typically pays 16 days late.
Due date is 2026-07-04.
revenueOpportunities.messageDraft: Hi Acme Retail Group, a quick note on INV-4012. Could you confirm the payment date so we can keep our schedule aligned?

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Queue an invoice follow-up and add a contact note with the requested payment-date confirmation.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner can soften the tone for sensitive customer relationships.

revenueOpportunities.title: Reconcile unmatched MockShopify order
revenueOpportunities.recommendedAction: Money was collected outside Xero. Create the contact and sales invoice so the payment can be reconciled and reported.
evidence: MockShopify order SHOPIFY-ORDER-1120 is paid (£720) with no confident Xero contact match.
Buyer "Unknown buyer" has no email on record and could not be matched above the 58% confidence floor.
Unrecorded sales understate revenue and break bank reconciliation until they are booked.
revenueOpportunities.messageDraft: Internal action: create a Xero contact for "Unknown buyer" and raise a £720 sales invoice matching SHOPIFY-ORDER-1120, then reconcile the incoming payment against it.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Prepare a new contact draft and a matching sales invoice so the external payment can be reconciled in Xero.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms the buyer identity and tax treatment before any record is created.

productivitySummary.highestImpactTask: Contractor payment prep with cash-risk check

productivityTasks.title: Contractor payment prep with cash-risk check
productivityTasks.workflow: Contractor spreadsheet -> bill allocation -> payroll sensitivity check -> approval batch
productivityTasks.businessImpact: Turns a messy contractor spreadsheet into a reviewed £7,600 payment batch.
messySignals: Spreadsheet has line-level contractor amounts but no Xero contact IDs.
Payment falls inside the cash risk window.
Payroll and contractor trust risk is high, so automation must not delay it silently.
automationSteps: Extract contractor categories and total from spreadsheet text.
Match total to the authorised Xero contractor bill.
Flag cash-flow risk before payment date.
Prepare an approval checklist rather than auto-paying.
productivityTasks.recommendedAction: Approve the payment batch only after reviewing cash impact and contractor priority.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Queue a reviewed payment-prep task with bill allocation, contractor notes, and cash-risk warning.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms payment timing; the app does not execute payroll or contractor payments automatically.

productivityTasks.title: Receipt to expense and bill match
productivityTasks.workflow: Receipt -> supplier bill -> expense account -> attachment -> approval
productivityTasks.businessImpact: Avoids manual entry and attaches evidence to a £2,100 supplier bill.
messySignals: Supplier name appears as Print Co. Ltd while Xero contact is PrintCo Supplies.
Receipt has reference 188 but no exact Xero bill URL.
automationSteps: Extract vendor, amount, due date, and reference from receipt text.
Fuzzy-match vendor and amount to authorised Xero bill.
Suggest Printing & production account code and attach receipt to the bill.
Queue reviewed bill-coding update for human approval.
productivityTasks.recommendedAction: Approve the suggested bill attachment and expense coding.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Attach the receipt, set the suggested expense account, and add a reviewed coding note to the Xero bill.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner or bookkeeper confirms the supplier match before the app updates the bill coding.

productivityTasks.title: Smart payment reconciliation
productivityTasks.sourceRecord: Bank feed transaction - ACME RETAIL GRP
productivityTasks.workflow: Bank feed -> payment match -> invoice close -> cash forecast refresh
productivityTasks.businessImpact: £4,200 can be matched to the open invoice without manual search.
messySignals: Bank memo uses ACME RETAIL GRP while Xero contact is Acme Retail Group.
Invoice reference is typed as INV4012 without the hyphen.
automationSteps: Normalise bank-feed memo text and invoice references.
Match payer, amount, and invoice number to authorised receivable.
Queue reconciliation and payment allocation against the Xero invoice.
Refresh the cash forecast after owner approval.
productivityTasks.recommendedAction: Approve the payment-to-invoice match and close the receivable.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Create a reviewed reconciliation task that allocates the bank receipt to the Xero invoice payment record.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms the bank transaction is not a duplicate before marking the invoice paid.

productivityTasks.title: Duplicate supplier bill guard
productivityTasks.workflow: Supplier inbox -> duplicate check -> payment hold -> Xero bill note
productivityTasks.businessImpact: Prevents a possible duplicate £1,800 payment to a critical supplier.
messySignals: Supplier is written as Cloud Lane in email but CloudLane Hosting in Xero.
Email has no bill number, only amount and month.
Supplier is marked high sensitivity, so auto-delay is unsafe.
automationSteps: Compare vendor alias, amount, period, and due date against open Xero bills.
Flag likely duplicate instead of creating a second bill.
Suggest a payment hold note on the existing bill.
Escalate to human review because supplier continuity risk is high.
productivityTasks.recommendedAction: Review the duplicate warning before paying or importing the forwarded bill.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Add a duplicate-risk note to the existing Xero bill and prevent a second import unless approved.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner decides whether the forwarded bill is a duplicate, revised bill, or missing attachment.

productivityTasks.title: Subscription expense categorisation
productivityTasks.workflow: Card receipt -> supplier bundle split -> account code -> recurring spend report
productivityTasks.businessImpact: Keeps £950 of monthly software spend consistently coded and reportable.
messySignals: Receipt bundles multiple SaaS vendors into one card charge.
Previous coding alternated between Software and General.
automationSteps: Classify vendor bundle as operating software.
Map to the existing recurring cash-flow category.
Suggest account coding and tracking category.
Queue a monthly control check for unusual increases.
productivityTasks.recommendedAction: Approve consistent software coding and recurring spend monitoring.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Apply the reviewed account-code suggestion and add this vendor bundle to recurring spend monitoring.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms the bundle is a business expense and not a personal card charge.

integrationSummary.topSyncAction: Prepare a Xero repeating-invoice template from the SaaS renewal.

integrationCandidates.title: SaaS renewal -> Xero repeating invoice
integrationCandidates.rawSignal: Retainer Wave renewal accepted: support plan 3,200 monthly, starts 25 July, customer alias Bright+Ops.
integrationCandidates.syncAction: Prepare a Xero repeating-invoice template from the SaaS renewal.

fieldMappings.mappedValue: Brightside Studios or new operations contact

resilienceNotes: Understands the phrase monthly as recurring cadence.
Maps alias Bright+Ops to a likely Xero contact but requests confirmation.
Converts renewal into draft repeating-invoice template rather than a one-off invoice.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Prepare a draft Xero repeating invoice template with cadence, amount, line item, and contact candidate.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms billing contact and recurring terms before creating the template.

integrationCandidates.title: Contractor sheet -> Xero supplier bill
integrationCandidates.rawSignal: Creative team July: Sam design 3200, Jo copy 2100, QA support 2300. No supplier bill IDs.
integrationCandidates.syncAction: Transform contractor rows into a reviewed Xero bill allocation.
resilienceNotes: Handles spreadsheet rows without requiring a rigid column template.
Aggregates line items to the authorised Xero contractor bill.
Routes missing supplier IDs to review instead of creating duplicates.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Prepare bill line allocations and a payment-prep checklist for the existing Xero contractor bill.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms supplier IDs and bank account before any payment workflow proceeds.

integrationCandidates.title: Won CRM deal -> Xero draft invoice
integrationCandidates.rawSignal: Brightside Studio Ltd / Conversion sprint / closed-won / 6.5k / invoice after kickoff / owner: Mia
integrationCandidates.syncAction: Prepare a draft Xero invoice from the won CRM deal.
resilienceNotes: Understands 6.5k as a currency amount instead of requiring a numeric field.
Maps CRM stage to a draft invoice, not an authorised invoice.
Uses existing Xero contact despite legal suffix and pluralisation differences.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Create a draft Xero invoice with mapped line item, contact, amount, and tracking category for review.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms scope, tax treatment, and invoice timing before the draft invoice is sent.

integrationCandidates.title: Shopify-style order -> Xero sales invoice
integrationCandidates.rawSignal: Harbor Coffee Co ordered Launch Kit + Brand Assets. Address missing VAT field. Paid by card.
integrationCandidates.syncAction: Convert paid order into a reviewed Xero sales invoice.
resilienceNotes: Does not fail when the order lacks VAT metadata; it routes the tax field to review.
Maps product bundle to multiple Xero line items.
Recognises Harbor Coffee as an existing Xero customer from partial name.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Create a draft paid sales invoice and queue the missing tax field for owner review.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms VAT/tax treatment before the order is synced into Xero.

integrationCandidates.title: Stripe payout -> invoice and fee mapping
integrationCandidates.rawSignal: Payout 1,912.58; gross 1,967.00; fee 54.42; description LUNA monthly content
integrationCandidates.syncAction: Split gross receipt and payment fee for Xero reconciliation.
resilienceNotes: Separates gross revenue from payment processor fee.
Uses fuzzy description matching to map LUNA to Luna Fitness.
Prepares reconciliation instead of forcing a one-line bank transaction.

approvalPlan.approvedExecution: Queue a Xero reconciliation split: gross revenue to invoice/payment, fee to bank fees expense.
approvalPlan.humanControl: Owner confirms fee treatment and invoice match before reconciliation is posted.

payload.note: 30-day crunch probability calculated from deterministic cash-flow simulation.

payload.explanation: Brightside Studio Ltd matched to Brightside Studios using normalised name and email domain.

ownerPriorities.title: Avoid the 18 Jul cash squeeze
ownerPriorities.practicalImpact: Baseline cash drops to -£2,650; approved actions lift the low point by £13,933.
ownerPriorities.recommendedMove: Approve "Offer Brightside Studios a 2% early-payment incentive" first, then review the remaining queued actions.
ownerPriorities.ownerOutcome: Protect payroll, rent, and supplier commitments before the gap appears.

ownerPriorities.title: Chase cash without sounding desperate
ownerPriorities.practicalImpact: Acme Retail Group can move £4,200 into the risk window.
ownerPriorities.recommendedMove: Send the drafted follow-up, then ask for a specific payment date rather than a vague update.
ownerPriorities.ownerOutcome: Reduce awkward collections work while keeping the customer relationship intact.

ownerPriorities.title: Win back revenue that already converted
ownerPriorities.practicalImpact: Harbor Coffee Co has proven buying history and is worth £5,460 in expected revenue.
ownerPriorities.recommendedMove: Send a personalised reactivation note tied to their last successful project.
ownerPriorities.ownerOutcome: Grow revenue without starting from a cold lead list.

ownerPriorities.title: Turn repeat work into a retainer
ownerPriorities.practicalImpact: Luna Fitness repeatedly buys Content; packaging it can add £1,967 of cash-flow lift.
ownerPriorities.recommendedMove: Offer a simple monthly plan with a clear scope, review cadence, and cancellation option.
ownerPriorities.ownerOutcome: Replace ad-hoc invoices with a more predictable base of recurring revenue.

ownerPriorities.title: Protect supplier trust while buying time
ownerPriorities.practicalImpact: PrintCo Supplies can be delayed by 7 days, protecting £2,100 before the risk window.
ownerPriorities.recommendedMove: Use the supplier message only for lower-sensitivity suppliers and keep the new date specific.
ownerPriorities.ownerOutcome: Create breathing room without training key suppliers to distrust payment promises.

narrative.headline: Cash risk appears on 2026-07-18; growth and cash actions remove the immediate breach.
narrative.summary: Baseline cash falls to -£2,650. With the recommended actions, the minimum projected cash position improves to £11,283. The revenue agent also found 7 growth opportunities worth £25,450 in expected revenue.
narrative.boardLevelNarrative: The strongest growth move is "Invoice closed-won Conversion Sprint", with £6,500 of expected revenue impact. The cash forecast still matters: "Offer Brightside Studios a 2% early-payment incentive" protects £7,683 before the risk window.
assumptions: Customer payment timing is estimated from contact-level payment behaviour.
Revenue opportunities are detected from Xero invoices, contacts, payment history, and invoice line items.
Supplier bills are assumed to be paid on planned payment date unless an approved action changes it.
AI agents explain and draft actions; deterministic forecast code performs numerical calculations.
After recommended actions, no threshold breach appears in the selected horizon.

tooling.agentToolkitPattern: OpenAI Agents SDK + MCPServerStdio pattern from XeroAPI/xero-agent-toolkit
tooling.scopeMode: 2026 granular scopes plus reports.read compatibility for BankSummary
tooling.safeWriteMode: No Xero write endpoint is called until a human approves the queued action.

xero.note: Seeded Xero-like demo data. Connect OAuth to replace this with live Accounting API data.

specialists.name: Productivity Automation Agent
specialists.role: Handles messy receipts, reconciliation, duplicate bills, and contractor payment prep.

specialists.name: Adaptive Integration Agent
specialists.role: Maps CRM, e-commerce, payments, SaaS, and spreadsheet data into Xero objects.

specialists.name: Data Quality Agent
specialists.role: Checks whether Xero data is complete enough for forecasting.

specialists.name: Forecast Agent
specialists.role: Interprets deterministic risk windows and Monte Carlo output.

specialists.name: Cash Action Agent
specialists.role: Ranks customer and supplier interventions by forecast impact.

specialists.name: Revenue Growth Agent
specialists.role: Finds dormant customers, upsells, subscription conversions, and service opportunities.

specialists.name: Outreach Agent
specialists.role: Turns revenue opportunities into customer-specific messages.

specialists.name: Supplier Payment Agent
specialists.role: Suggests which bills are safe to delay with human approval.

specialists.name: CFO Narrative Agent
specialists.role: Explains the forecast in plain English.

traceSteps.reasoning: Checked whether due dates, contacts, opening cash, and payment history are complete enough to trust the forecast.

traceSteps.input: 90-day ledger with 90 Monte Carlo band points
traceSteps.reasoning: Interpreted the risk window, safe-cash threshold, simulated uncertainty, and before/after action movement.
traceSteps.output: Cash falls below threshold on 2026-07-18; after-action minimum is £11,283.

traceSteps.reasoning: Ranked customer and supplier moves by cash protected before the risk window and relationship risk.
traceSteps.output: 3 pending cash action(s); 3 action(s) included in after-action forecast.

traceSteps.input: 7 pending revenue opportunities from invoices, contacts, line items, and external matches
traceSteps.reasoning: Looked for closed-won uninvoiced work, dormant high-value customers, repeat purchases, upsells, and unmatched external orders.
Kept outreach specific, respectful, and gated behind owner approval.
traceSteps.output: Draft messages are editable before any Xero writeback or external send.

agentLayer.traceHint: OPENAI_API_KEY is not set, so deterministic fallback text is active while agent scaffolding remains ready.
